jokes.text: Boy, they're really sockin' it to that Spiro Agnew guy again. He must work there or something.
jokes.setup: Looking at old Mad Magazine making fun of disgraced politician Spiro Agnew
jokes.punchline: Child assumes the magazine keeps mocking him because he must be an employee
jokes.explanation: Perfect child logic misunderstanding why magazines repeatedly target political figures - Bart's innocent assumption is character-appropriate and clever

jokes.text: You fold it, you bought it.
characters: Comic Book Guy or store clerk
jokes.setup: Kids folding Mad Magazine fold-in puzzle in store
jokes.punchline: Play on 'you break it, you bought it' policy
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that's economical and fits the situation perfectly

jokes.text: Does something smell funny in here? - I don't think so, stupid.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to practice snappy comebacks from Mad Magazine
jokes.punchline: Completely misses the point of the 'snappy answers' game
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to the concept while being confident about it is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Homie, you want pork chops? - No, I want roast beef, you clod!
jokes.setup: Homer still practicing 'snappy answers' inappropriately
jokes.punchline: Uses it rudely on his wife for a normal dinner question
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer completely missing social cues while obsessed with his magazine game

jokes.text: The 'all-ighty ollar'?
jokes.setup: Looking at Mad Magazine fold-in that reveals 'the almighty dollar'
jokes.punchline: Parts of letters are cut off by the fold creating nonsense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual pun that shows how magazine fold-ins can go wrong - highly quotable nonsense phrase

jokes.text: They never come around anymore now that they got their mistresses.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining why his regular customers don't come to the bar
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of extramarital affairs as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: Perfect for Moe's seedy worldview - treats adultery as mundane business concern

jokes.text: Sorry. It's league night. I couldn't give a lane to my own mother. - I have no son.
jokes.setup: Can't give lane even to own mother
jokes.punchline: Instantly disowns himself rather than face the contradiction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical absurdity - solves contradiction by denying his own existence. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: Well, I'm better than dirt. Well, most kinds of dirt. I mean, not that fancy, store-bought dirt. That stuff's loaded with nutrients. I can't compete with that stuff.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to build self-esteem by comparing himself to dirt
jokes.punchline: Realizes even dirt is better than him when he thinks about it
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer escalating self-deprecation into absurdity. Perfect character moment that became iconic - the dirt comparison is brilliantly specific and pathetic.

jokes.text: Oh, no problem. Would you take an out-of-state, two-party, bad check?
jokes.setup: Asked for $500 bowling registration fee
jokes.punchline: Offers most unreliable form of payment while acting like it's reasonable
jokes.explanation: Homer's financial irresponsibility delivered with complete confidence - the specificity makes it funnier

jokes.text: No, I will not pay you $500 for sex.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for money for bowling registration
jokes.punchline: Marge misinterprets the request completely
jokes.explanation: Sharp misdirection that reveals how Marge interprets Homer's schemes

jokes.text: I don't have that kind of money to spend on sex.
jokes.setup: Continuing the sex misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Implies she has SOME money for sex, just not $500 worth
jokes.explanation: Marge accidentally implies she has a sex budget - perfectly innocent character making it funnier

jokes.text: Just last week, I asked him for $1,500. - For what? - Oh, I gotta get the third degree from you too?
jokes.setup: Homer claims Burns never gives money to anybody
jokes.punchline: Refuses to explain what he wanted $1,500 for, acting defensive
jokes.explanation: Homer's defensiveness about unexplained requests fits his character

jokes.text: I'd better take off my sweater.
jokes.setup: Bart claiming it's hot in class
jokes.punchline: Reveals anti-homework t-shirt underneath
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart rebellion setup - the premeditation makes it funnier

jokes.text: His shirt makes a good point. I'm with the shirt!
jokes.setup: Students see Bart's anti-homework shirt
jokes.punchline: Student allies with the clothing item rather than Bart
jokes.explanation: Absurd personification of the shirt as the leader of the movement

jokes.text: I'll just write the zero first. Now a vertical line to indicate the one.
jokes.setup: Claiming to give the school a perfect 10
jokes.punchline: Actually writing 01, which is terrible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that uses the audience's assumption against them. Perfect timing.

jokes.text: Skinner, why are there children walking on my head?
jokes.setup: Students rioting outside
jokes.punchline: Chalmers' bizarre description of students on upper floor
jokes.explanation: Surreal way to describe the school layout that's both logical and absurd

jokes.text: The year was 1968. We were on recon in a steaming Mekong delta...
jokes.setup: Chalmers recognizing Mad Magazine reference
jokes.punchline: Launches into elaborate Vietnam War story about a t-shirt
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation from school discipline to war trauma over a magazine t-shirt

jokes.text: I spent the next three years in a P.O.W. camp forced to subsist on a thin stew made of fish, vegetables, prawns, coconut milk and four kinds of rice.
jokes.setup: Describing prison camp food as torture
jokes.punchline: Actually describes what sounds like delicious Thai cuisine
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - 'torture' that sounds like gourmet food. Perfect structure and earned laughs.

jokes.text: I came close to madness trying to find it here in the States, but they just can't get the spices right.
jokes.setup: Previous 'prison torture' food description
jokes.punchline: Reveals he actually loved the food and wants to find it again
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the prison stew joke - completely reverses our understanding

jokes.text: Pop 'n' Fresh, you glutinous little dough boy!
jokes.setup: Burns on ether sees Homer as Pillsbury Doughboy
jokes.punchline: Addresses him as the advertising mascot with twisted affection
jokes.explanation: Burns' drug-induced hallucination fits his character's corporate obsession

jokes.text: Hey, everybody, if you wanna ask Burns for a favor, now's the time! He's doped up or dyin' or somethin'!
jokes.setup: Burns acting strange on ether
jokes.punchline: Homer announces it to everyone like a sale at a store
jokes.explanation: Homer's opportunistic nature and public announcement style perfectly in character

jokes.text: Why, it's that delightful TV leprechaun! I'm going to get your Lucky Charms.
jokes.setup: Burns on drugs seeing Groundskeeper Willie
jokes.punchline: Mistakes him for Lucky the Leprechaun mascot
jokes.explanation: Another advertising mascot hallucination, fits the pattern

jokes.text: Oh, no. My brains.
jokes.setup: Burns attacking Willie thinking he's a leprechaun
jokes.punchline: Willie's understated reaction to brain injury
jokes.explanation: Willie's deadpan delivery of serious injury is perfect character moment

jokes.text: Hey, nice, quote, uniforms, end quote.
jokes.setup: Nelson making fun of the bowling team's outfits
jokes.punchline: Says 'quote' and 'end quote' out loud for sarcastic air quotes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about air quotes that became a cultural reference

jokes.text: This just in. A new addition to our worst-dressed list: those guys!
jokes.setup: Following Nelson's uniform insult
jokes.punchline: TV news treats local bowling team fashion as breaking news
jokes.explanation: Media absurdity escalation fits Kent's character perfectly

jokes.text: Oh, forget this. I am far too fragile to withstand an evening of barbs like that.
jokes.setup: Facing mild criticism about uniforms
jokes.punchline: Apu treats gentle teasing as unbearable assault
jokes.explanation: Apu's over-dramatic sensitivity fits his character's flowery speech

jokes.text: Buenas noches, senoritas!
jokes.setup: Spanish-speaking TV character appears
jokes.punchline: Calls the male bowlers 'senoritas' (young ladies)
jokes.explanation: Simple gender confusion joke that fits Bumblebee Man's character

jokes.text: Oh, man! I knew I was gonna choke.
jokes.setup: Otto worried about choking under pressure
jokes.punchline: Says it immediately after succeeding
jokes.explanation: Otto's obliviousness to his own success fits his character

jokes.text: That's a funny-lookin' strike.
jokes.setup: Otto makes an unusual but successful bowling shot
jokes.punchline: Moe's understated comment on the weird technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe deadpan delivery - accepting success while noting its strangeness

jokes.text: You can do it, Otto! Make this spare, I give you free gelato! Then back to my place, where I will get you blotto! Domo arigato, Mr. Roboto.
jokes.setup: Team cheer for Otto's bowling
jokes.punchline: Elaborate rhyming cheer ending with random Styx lyrics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating rhyme scheme with perfect pop culture reference payoff

jokes.text: Right away, my lips started to move and I came up with a chant that won the match!
jokes.setup: Homer claiming credit for the team's victory
jokes.punchline: Takes credit for the cheer rather than the actual bowling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer narcissism - taking credit for peripheral contribution

jokes.text: Who knocked down the pins? - I don't know. You know, some guy. Otto, I guess.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about the actual bowling performance
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses the person who actually won as irrelevant
jokes.explanation: Homer's dismissal of Otto shows his complete self-centeredness perfectly

jokes.text: The individual doesn't matter. It was a team effort. And I was the one who came up with the whole team idea. Me.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming teamwork is what matters
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by taking personal credit
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction that reveals Homer's psychology - pays lip service to teamwork while being purely selfish

jokes.text: No, no. Now, don't try to remember what that slogan was.
jokes.setup: Skinner referencing the t-shirt riot incident
jokes.punchline: Tells people not to remember, ensuring they will
jokes.explanation: Classic reverse psychology that Skinner doesn't understand he's using

jokes.text: Say hello to our little genius, Martin, who looks even smarter in this vest and short pant combination from Mr. Boy of Main Street.
jokes.setup: Presenting school uniform fashion show
jokes.punchline: Treating children's clothing like high fashion with made-up designer names
jokes.explanation: Skinner's attempt to make uniforms glamorous with fake fashion terminology

jokes.text: She'll have no reason to play the blues in this snappy ensemble topped off with a saucy French beret that seems to scream 'silence.'
jokes.setup: Describing Lisa's uniform positively
jokes.punchline: Beret 'screams silence' - contradiction in terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contradiction - something can't scream silence. Perfect Skinner obliviousness.

jokes.text: Go, Moe! Go, Moe! Don't make Homer shout out, 'D'oh!'
jokes.setup: Homer cheering for Moe's bowling
jokes.punchline: References his own catchphrase in the cheer
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke where Homer references his own famous catchphrase within the show

jokes.text: One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, 10. Bye!
jokes.setup: Snake bowling for the Pin Pals
jokes.punchline: Counts his footsteps instead of following through, casual exit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Snake character moment - treating bowling like a heist with casual criminal attitude

jokes.text: The Holy Rollers have won it five years in a row. They think they're so high and mighty just 'cause they never got caught drivin' without pants.
jokes.setup: Discussing the championship bowling team
jokes.punchline: Their moral superiority is based on avoiding a very specific crime
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that reveals Moe's criminal worldview - assumes everyone drives pantless sometimes

jokes.text: Yeah, Moe, that team sure did suck last night. They just plain sucked. I've seen teams suck before, but they were the suckiest bunch of sucks that ever sucked.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on bad bowling team performance
jokes.punchline: Escalating repetition of 'suck' to absurd degree
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke that became highly quotable - Homer's crude vocabulary limitations

jokes.text: Homer, watch your mouth! - I gotta go. My damn wiener kids are listening.
jokes.setup: Marge telling Homer to watch his language
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately curses while referring to his children crudely
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction of Marge's request with additional inappropriate language

jokes.text: We are not wieners. - Then what are you dressed like that for? - They made us. - Oh. 'They made us.' That's loser talk.
jokes.setup: Kids defending their uniforms
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses valid excuse as weak mentality
jokes.explanation: Homer's toxic masculinity applied to school uniform policy - unreasonable but in character

jokes.text: Sir, that's a check for your boweling. - Oh, yes. That's very important.
jokes.setup: Burns confused about a check he wrote
jokes.punchline: Mishears 'bowling' as 'boweling' (defecation)
jokes.explanation: Burns' age-related mishearing creates uncomfortable bodily function humor

jokes.text: Yes, sir. Remember that month you didn't do it? - Yes, that was unpleasant for all concerned.
jokes.setup: Continuing the boweling misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Both discussing Burns' constipation as office matter
jokes.explanation: Escalates the bathroom humor while maintaining the characters' formal tone

jokes.text: Oh, yes. That greedy, grasping glob of chemicals.
jokes.setup: Burns remembering Homer as the Pillsbury Doughboy
jokes.punchline: Describes the beloved mascot as evil corporate entity
jokes.explanation: Burns projects his own corporate evil onto innocent mascot with great alliteration

jokes.text: Look at them, Smithers, enjoying their embezzlement. - I have a much uglier word for it, sir: misappropriation.
jokes.setup: Burns calling Homer's bowling 'embezzlement'
jokes.punchline: Smithers thinks 'misappropriation' is the uglier word
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers moment - thinks the more bureaucratic term is somehow worse. Brilliant character comedy.

jokes.text: I've had one of my unpredictable changes of heart.
jokes.setup: Burns suddenly wanting to join the bowling team
jokes.punchline: Calls his whims 'unpredictable' as if they're a known trait
jokes.explanation: Burns acknowledges his own inconsistency as if it were a charming quirk rather than a character flaw

jokes.text: Yeah. Call this an unfair generalization if you must, but old people are no good at everything.
jokes.setup: Team worried about Burns joining
jokes.punchline: Apu's polite disclaimer followed by sweeping ageist statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu politeness contrasted with brutal honesty. The 'if you must' qualifier makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It wasn't any easier.
jokes.setup: Homer earlier said it would be easier to reject Burns after seeing him bowl
jokes.punchline: Admits his plan failed completely
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with excellent economy - two words that explain Homer's complete failure of nerve

jokes.text: Horseplay's down 40%. Youthful exuberance has been cut in half. High spirits are at an all-time low.
jokes.setup: Skinner praising the effect of school uniforms
jokes.punchline: Lists crushing of joy as positive achievements
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Skinner treating the destruction of childhood happiness as statistical victories

jokes.text: They've even begun blinking in unison.
jokes.setup: Continuing praise of uniform effects
jokes.punchline: Students now synchronized like robots or prisoners
jokes.explanation: Creepy detail that shows how far the conformity has gone - dystopian but delivered cheerfully

jokes.text: Oh, there's something I used to do in this situation, but can't remember. Ha- ho?
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to remember how to express herself
jokes.punchline: Can barely remember how to laugh, makes broken sound
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dystopian moment - Lisa forgetting how to laugh shows the horrifying effect of conformity

jokes.text: Whoo! They begged me to join their team- begged me.
jokes.setup: Burns after terrible bowling performance
jokes.punchline: His delusion about being wanted despite clearly ruining the team
jokes.explanation: Good Burns delusion showing his complete disconnect from reality

jokes.text: The only ship worth a damn is friendship.
jokes.setup: Burns dismissing the championship
jokes.punchline: Corny pun about 'ship' while being completely uncharacteristic
jokes.explanation: Burns attempting to be sentimental is funny because it's so out of character

jokes.text: Oops. Lost a nail. Well, that's leprosy for you.
jokes.setup: Burns losing a fingernail
jokes.punchline: Casually attributes it to having leprosy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - Burns treats serious disease as minor inconvenience. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: Wow! Look at all the colors, man! Red, green, yellow, orange! I remember all of these!
jokes.setup: Rain makes the cheap uniforms run colors
jokes.punchline: Otto acts like he's rediscovering colors after gray uniform period
jokes.explanation: Otto's hippie personality treating color return as psychedelic experience

jokes.text: I got them at the same place I buy Mother's dresses, and- - Good Lord! Mother's in the park!
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining where he bought uniforms
jokes.punchline: Realizes his mother is outside in dissolving clothes
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Skinner's cheap shopping affects both school and his elderly mother

jokes.text: So you're saying we're definitely gonna win? Whoo-hoo! I won't need this anymore. [breaks trophy case]
jokes.setup: Marge saying maybe they'll win the championship
jokes.punchline: Homer misinterprets 'maybe' as certainty and destroys trophy case
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - hearing what he wants and acting impulsively with permanent consequences

jokes.text: Don't worry about nothin', Homer. I have a feeling that Mr. Burns is gonna have a little accident that might keep him from bowlin' with us tonight, heh, heh, heh.
jokes.setup: Homer worried about Burns ruining their chances
jokes.punchline: Moe implies he'll hurt Burns to prevent him from playing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment - casual violence as problem-solving

jokes.text: D'oh! He's gonna ruin everything!
jokes.setup: Burns recovers from injury and can still play
jokes.punchline: Homer's catchphrase as Moe's plan backfires
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer reaction with his catchphrase fitting the situation

jokes.text: At last, I finally have a garment fine enough to be married in.
jokes.setup: Apu admiring his bowling shirt
jokes.punchline: Considers polyester bowling shirt formal wear
jokes.explanation: Apu's immigrant perspective on American fashion creates endearing moment

jokes.text: I've always been wealthy, but this is the first time I ever felt... rich.
jokes.setup: Burns wearing bowling shirt
jokes.punchline: Distinguishes between wealth and feeling rich from friendship
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly touching moment that shows Burns' emotional poverty despite material wealth

jokes.text: Now I must discard my teammates, much like the boxer must shed roll after roll of sweaty, useless, disgusting flab before he can win the title.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining why he's abandoning the team
jokes.punchline: Compares his friends to disgusting body fat that must be removed
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly awful Burns metaphor that reveals his complete contempt for human connection

jokes.text: I guess some people never change. Or they quickly change and then quickly change back.
jokes.setup: Burns' betrayal after seeming friendship
jokes.punchline: Apu's philosophical observation about Burns' consistency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu wisdom - finds the pattern in Burns' apparently random behavior

jokes.text: Well, I guess no one can. - Run, before they're through feeding!
jokes.setup: Homer's earlier cheer 'if you can't do it, nobody can' is proven right
jokes.punchline: Team flees as if Homer is a dangerous animal
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that treats Homer's failure as literally dangerous to witness
